observation: windows.netscan shows local sockets on this host bound to two distinct private IPv4 addresses: 10.10.6.15 (e.g. ncpa_listener. LISTENING on 10.10.6.15:5693; java.exe ESTABLISHED 10.10.6.15:49203 -> 10.10.200.207:5672) and 172.16.6.15 (e.g. System NetBIOS UDP on 172.16.6.15:137 and :138; and 172.16.6.15:3262 ESTABLISHED to 172.16.5.50). svchost.exe PID 392 is LISTENING on 0.0.0.0:3389 (TCPv4 and TCPv6) and System (PID 4) is LISTENING on 0.0.0.0:445.

windows.netscan shows PID 22456 'subject_srv.ex' LISTENING on 0.0.0.0:3262 (TCPv4 offset 0xbbe3f620, plus the matching TCPv6 ::3262), and an ESTABLISHED TCPv4 session 172.16.6.15:3262 with foreign address 172.16.5.50:43036 (offset 0xbbf88a30) owned by the same PID 22456.

reason: [seat:inference] Inference overreach (CERTAINTY): unjustified certainty — a single artifact rarely proves a conclusion outright. Offending clause: "CAVEAT: netscan proves only the live socket and its single internal peer".
